description: Traditional rulers and their documented political interventions, 2011–2026.

data.title: Sultan of Sokoto
data.role: President-General, Nigerian Supreme Council for Islamic Affairs

documented_interventions.description: Called for calm during post-election violence in the north following Jonathan's victory
Urged Nigerians to accept election results; called for peace

data.notes: The Sultan is the most senior Muslim traditional ruler in Nigeria. His political statements carry national weight but he typically avoids overt partisan endorsements.

data.title: Ooni of Ife
data.notes: The Ooni commands significant symbolic authority in Yoruba politics. Not documented as making partisan endorsements.

data.title: Oba of Lagos

documented_interventions.description: Made a controversial statement warning Igbo residents of Lagos about voting consequences — widely condemned; Oba later apologized

data.notes: The Oba of Lagos is historically aligned with Lagos APC politics.

data.title: Northern Traditional Rulers Council
data.role: Collective body of northern emirs and chiefs
data.notes: Individual emirs (Emir of Kano, Emir of Katsina, etc.) have varying levels of political influence. The deposition and reinstatement of Emir Sanusi Lamido Sanusi in Kano (2020) is a documented political flashpoint.

gaps.note: This is a preliminary stub. Full documentation requires fetching news archives. Particularly important: any traditional ruler statements on 2027 election or candidate endorsements.
